prompt:
The nav logo "Yilan Gao" is wrapping to two lines when the viewport gets
squeezed. This should never be collapsed or moved to the next line. It
should never compromise; this is a principle. Anything that's logo or
navigation, such a core identity line, should never ever be returned to
two lines when it's squeezed. The personal identity blurb, which is the
'Designer, AI systems experience...', that line CAN be squeezed into two
lines — that's fine. Fix the nav and codify the priority rule.
response:
## Diagnosis The nav logo "Yilan Gao" is wrapping because the flex container is distributing space without protecting the logo from shrinking. The logo text lacks `whitespace-nowrap` and/or `flex-shrink-0`, so when the viewport narrows, the flex algorithm treats the logo as a compressible item — it wraps before it clips. The identity blurb beside it has no explicit permission to absorb the squeeze instead. **Anti-pattern: AP-072** — Flex children without explicit shrink budgets, causing layout collapse of identity-critical elements. --- ## Root Cause In a flex nav row, all children share `flex-shrink: 1` by default. The logo has no protection, so it wraps. The correct model is: - Logo: `flex-shrink: 0` + `whitespace-nowrap` — **never compresses, never wraps** - Identity blurb: `flex-shrink: 1` + `min-w-0` — **absorbs all squeeze** - Nav links: `flex-shrink: 0` + `whitespace-nowrap` — **never wraps** --- ## Fix ### 1. Core layout constraint — `src/components/nav/Nav.tsx` ```tsx // src/components/nav/Nav.tsx export function Nav() { return (
{/* LEFT CLUSTER — logo + identity blurb */}
{/* LOGO — identity-critical: NEVER wraps, NEVER shrinks */} Yilan Gao {/* IDENTITY BLURB — MAY wrap; absorbs all remaining squeeze */} Designer, AI systems experience & strategy
{/* RIGHT CLUSTER — nav links: also identity-critical, never wrap */}
  • Work
  • About
  • Contact
) } ``` ### 2. Nav shell — `src/styles/components/nav.css` Codify the priority rule directly in CSS as a named constraint layer, not just a utility. This makes the rule auditable and grep-able. ```css /* src/styles/components/nav.css */ /* ───────────────────────────────────────────── NAV SHELL Priority model: 1. Logo → never shrinks, never wraps (flex-shrink: 0) 2. Nav links → never shrinks, never wraps (flex-shrink: 0) 3. Identity blurb → absorbs all squeeze (flex-shrink: 1) ───────────────────────────────────────────── */ .nav-shell { display: flex; align-items: baseline; justify-content: space-between; gap: var(--space-6); padding-inline: var(--space-6); padding-block: var(--space-4); } /* ── IDENTITY-CRITICAL ELEMENTS ───────────────── These must NEVER be compressed, wrapped, or visually degraded. This is a design principle, not a preference. Any element marked [data-identity-critical] enforces this contract. ─────────────────────────────────────────────── */ [data-identity-critical] { flex-shrink: 0; white-space: nowrap; min-width: max-content; /* belt-and-suspenders: never narrower than text */ } /* ── NAV LOGO ─────────────────────────────────── The primary identity mark. Inherits the identity-critical contract above. ─────────────────────────────────────────────── */ .nav-logo { font-family: var(--font-display); font-size: var(--text-lg); font-weight: var(--font-semibold); letter-spacing: var(--tracking-tight); color: var(--color-foreground); text-decoration: none; } /* ── IDENTITY BLURB ───────────────────────────── Secondary descriptor. MAY wrap at narrow viewports — this is intentional and acceptable. ─────────────────────────────────────────────── */ .nav-identity-blurb { font-size: var(--text-sm); color: var(--color-muted); min-width: 0; /* permits shrink below intrinsic size */ flex-shrink: 1; /* explicit: this is the designated squeeze target */ } ``` ### 3. Apply the `data-identity-critical` attribute in markup ```tsx // src/components/nav/Nav.tsx — updated anchor Yilan Gao {/* Nav links wrapper — also identity-critical */}
    ...
``` ### 4. Codify the rule in a design token comment — `src/styles/tokens/layout.css` ```css /* src/styles/tokens/layout.css */ /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────── LAYOUT PRIORITY PRINCIPLE — NAV ───────────────────────────────────────────────── Rule: Identity-critical elements hold at all costs. NEVER wraps: logo text, nav link text MAY wrap: identity blurb, page subtitles NEVER hides: logo, primary nav links MAY hide: secondary nav items at xs (hamburger pattern) Implementation contract: - Identity-critical → [data-identity-critical] attribute - CSS enforces: flex-shrink: 0; white-space: nowrap - This is NOT a responsive breakpoint decision. It applies at ALL viewport widths without exception. ───────────────────────────────────────────────── */ ``` --- ## Principle **Identity-critical elements hold at all costs.** The logo and nav links are not negotiable layout participants — they are constants. In a flex nav, the squeeze budget belongs entirely to secondary content (the blurb). This is enforced via a named CSS contract (`[data-identity-critical]`) rather than ad-hoc utilities, so the rule is auditable, searchable, and impossible to silently violate by removing a class. The blurb wrapping at narrow viewports is not a bug — it's the designated behavior of the designated compressible element.
